heading: Transfer without negotiation
label: 80106 Transfer without negotiation
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevisedSectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)80106(a)49 App.:109 (1st sentence)., §§ 29 (1st sentence), 32, 33, .Aug. 29, 1916, ch. 41539 Stat. 54349 App.:112 (1st sentence).80106(b)49 App.:113.80106(c)49 App.:112 (2d–last sentences).In subsection (a), the words “without negotiating it” are added for clarity.In subsection (b), the text of 49 App.:113 (last sentence) is omitted as unnecessary because of the words “the transferee may compel the transferor”.In subsection (c)(1), before clause (A), the words “also acquires the right to notify” and “by the transferor or transferee of a straight bill” are omitted as unnecessary because of the restatement.
amendments: Historical and Revision NotesRevisedSectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)80106(a)49 App.:109 (1st sentence)., §§ 29 (1st sentence), 32, 33, .Aug. 29, 1916, ch. 41539 Stat. 54349 App.:112 (1st sentence).80106(b)49 App.:113.80106(c)49 App.:112 (2d–last sentences).In subsection (a), the words “without negotiating it” are added for clarity.In subsection (b), the text of 49 App.:113 (last sentence) is omitted as unnecessary because of the words “the transferee may compel the transferor”.In subsection (c)(1), before clause (A), the words “also acquires the right to notify” and “by the transferor or transferee of a straight bill” are omitted as unnecessary because of the restatement.

children.heading: .—Delivery and Agreement
children.label: a .—Delivery and Agreement
children.text: The holder of a bill of lading may transfer the bill without negotiating it by delivery and agreement to transfer title to the bill or to the goods represented by it. Subject to the agreement, the person to whom the bill is transferred has title to the goods against the transferor.

children.heading: .—Compelling Indorsement
children.label: b .—Compelling Indorsement
children.text: When a negotiable bill of lading is transferred for value by delivery without being negotiated and indorsement of the transferor is essential for negotiation, the transferee may compel the transferor to indorse the bill unless a contrary intention appears. The negotiation is effective when the indorsement is made.

children.label: A
children.text: garnishment, attachment, or execution on the goods by a creditor of the transferor; or

children.label: B
children.text: notice to the carrier by the transferor or a purchaser from the transferor of a later purchase of the goods from the transferor.

children.label: A
children.text: an officer or agent of the carrier, whose actual or apparent authority includes acting on the notification, has been notified; and

children.label: B
children.text: the officer or agent has had time, exercising reasonable diligence, to communicate with the agent having possession or control of the goods.
